data.title: Does Ashtead Group (LON:AHT) Have A Healthy Balance Sheet?
data.description:
Some say volatility, rather than debt, is the best way to think about risk as an investor, but Warren Buffett famously said that 'Volatility is far from synonymous with risk.' When we think about how risky a company is, we always like to look at its use of debt, since debt overload can lead to ruin. We note that Ashtead Group plc (LON:AHT) does have debt on its balance sheet. But is this debt a concern to shareholders?

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Risk Does Debt Bring?

Debt and other liabilities become risky for a business when it cannot easily fulfill those obligations, either with free cash flow or by raising capital at an attractive price. If things get really bad, the lenders can take control of the business. However, a more frequent (but still costly) occurrence is where a company must issue shares at bargain-basement prices, permanently diluting shareholders, just to shore up its balance sheet. Of course, plenty of companies use debt to fund growth, without any negative consequences. The first step when considering a company's debt levels is to consider its cash and debt together.

What Is Ashtead Group's Net Debt?

As you can see below, Ashtead Group had US$7.68b of debt at October 2025, down from US$8.19b a year prior. Net debt is about the same, since the it doesn't have much cash.

How Healthy Is Ashtead Group's Balance Sheet?

We can see from the most recent balance sheet that Ashtead Group had liabilities of US$1.86b falling due within a year, and liabilities of US$12.8b due beyond that. Offsetting these obligations, it had cash of US$39.6m as well as receivables valued at US$2.18b due within 12 months. So its liabilities total US$12.4b more than the combination of its cash and short-term receivables.

While this might seem like a lot, it is not so bad since Ashtead Group has a huge market capitalization of US$29.5b, and so it could probably strengthen its balance sheet by raising capital if it needed to. However, it is still worthwhile taking a close look at its ability to pay off debt.

See our latest analysis for Ashtead Group

We use two main ratios to inform us about debt levels relative to earnings. The first is net debt divided by earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA), while the second is how many times its earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) covers its interest expense (or its interest cover, for short). This way, we consider both the absolute quantum of the debt, as well as the interest rates paid on it.

Ashtead Group's net debt is sitting at a very reasonable 1.6 times its EBITDA, while its EBIT covered its interest expense just 4.6 times last year. While these numbers do not alarm us, it's worth noting that the cost of the company's debt is having a real impact. Sadly, Ashtead Group's EBIT actually dropped 5.3% in the last year. If that earnings trend continues then its debt load will grow heavy like the heart of a polar bear watching its sole cub. There's no doubt that we learn most about debt from the balance sheet. But it is future earnings, more than anything, that will determine Ashtead Group's ability to maintain a healthy balance sheet going forward. So if you're focused on the future you can check out this free report showing analyst profit forecasts.

Finally, while the tax-man may adore accounting profits, lenders only accept cold hard cash. So it's worth checking how much of that EBIT is backed by free cash flow. In the last three years, Ashtead Group's free cash flow amounted to 42% of its EBIT, less than we'd expect. That's not great, when it comes to paying down debt.

Our View

Both Ashtead Group's EBIT growth rate and its interest cover were discouraging. But its not so bad at managing its debt, based on its EBITDA,. Looking at all the angles mentioned above, it does seem to us that Ashtead Group is a somewhat risky investment as a result of its debt. Not all risk is bad, as it can boost share price returns if it pays off, but this debt risk is worth keeping in mind. The balance sheet is clearly the area to focus on when you are analysing debt. But ultimately, every company can contain risks that exist outside of the balance sheet. We've identified 1 warning sign with Ashtead Group , and understanding them should be part of your investment process.

Of course, if you're the type of investor who prefers buying stocks without the burden of debt, then don't hesitate to discover our exclusive list of net cash growth stocks, today.

data.title: Should Leviathan Expansion and Venezuela Push Reshape Chevron’s (CVX) Geopolitical Risk and Cash Flow Mix?
data.description:
• Chevron and its partners previously reached a final investment decision to expand the Leviathan natural gas field offshore Israel, aiming to double production capacity to about 21 billion cubic meters per year through new wells, subsea infrastructure, and platform upgrades to support regional energy security for Israel, Egypt, and Jordan.
  • At the same time, Chevron’s push for an expanded U.S. license in Venezuela could materially reshape its mix of gas and heavy oil assets across politically sensitive regions.
  • Next, we’ll examine how the Leviathan expansion decision could influence Chevron’s long-term cash flow profile and overall investment narrative.

Chevron Investment Narrative Recap

To own Chevron, you need to be comfortable with a global, oil and gas focused business that is still heavily dependent on large upstream projects and commodity prices. The Leviathan expansion and potential Venezuela license both reinforce that high capital intensity and geopolitical exposure remain central to the story, but they do not fundamentally change the near term catalyst of delivering cost reductions and disciplined project execution, or the key risk of long term demand pressure from the energy transition.

Among recent announcements, the confirmation that Chevron expects 2025 production growth at the top end of its 6% to 8% guidance stands out next to the Leviathan decision. Together, they underline how much of the investment case hinges on turning a growing production base into resilient cash generation, even as earnings and margins have recently softened and capital spending commitments in places like the Eastern Mediterranean and Venezuela stay elevated.

Yet investors should also weigh how Chevron’s growing exposure to politically sensitive regions could affect...

data.title: How FDA Review of Oclaiz Could Shape Camurus’ (OM:CAMX) Long-Term Endocrinology Strategy
data.description:
• Camurus AB (publ) recently announced that the U.S. FDA has accepted for review its resubmitted New Drug Application for Oclaiz (CAM2029), an extended-release octreotide injection for acromegaly, with a PDUFA target action date set for 10 June 2026.
  • This acceptance helps clear a prior hurdle tied to third-party manufacturing observations and follows earlier EU and UK approvals and launch of the same product, marketed there as Oczyesa.
  • We will now examine how FDA acceptance of the Oclaiz resubmission, supported by Phase 3 ACROINNOVA data, may influence Camurus’s investment narrative.

Advertisement

Camurus Investment Narrative Recap

To own Camurus, you need to believe in its ability to grow long-acting injectable therapies across addiction and endocrine disorders while broadening its pipeline beyond a few core products. The FDA’s acceptance of the Oclaiz resubmission reduces a key U.S. regulatory uncertainty for CAM2029 in acromegaly and makes the timing of the PDUFA decision in June 2026 the main near term catalyst, while execution risk around Brixadi and Buvidal and exposure to reimbursement and currency headwinds remain central concerns.

Among recent updates, the UK and EU approvals and initial launch of Oczyesa for acromegaly in 2025 are most relevant here, because they show that CAM2029 is already an approved and commercial product in other major markets. That context frames the U.S. NDA acceptance as less about clinical or safety questions and more about resolving prior manufacturing issues, with potential implications for how investors weigh regulatory risk across the broader CAM2029 program including its PLD indication.

data.title: A Look At Revvity (RVTY) Valuation After 2025 Guidance Update And Eli Lilly AI Collaboration
data.description:

See our latest analysis for Revvity.

Revvity's shares have rebounded in the short term, with a 30 day share price return of 16.9% and a 7 day gain of 8.29%. However, longer term total shareholder returns remain weak, including a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 4.71%, which suggests recent momentum is only starting to offset a tougher few years.

If the combination of AI and diagnostics interests you, it could be worth widening your search to other healthcare stocks that may fit a similar theme.

With Revvity guiding to steady 2025 revenue, trading around US$112.50 and sitting at roughly a 13% estimated intrinsic discount and only a small 3% discount to analyst targets, is there still a buying opportunity here or is future growth already priced in?

Revvity's most followed narrative puts fair value at US$114.63, only slightly above the last close of US$112.50. This frames the situation as a fairly tight valuation gap built on specific growth and margin assumptions.

Ongoing shift in product mix toward higher margin, software enabled and consumables driven offerings (e.g., SaaS Signals, reagents, new IDS i20 platform), along with structural cost actions, are expected to materially expand operating and net margins, with 2026 set to start at a higher 28% operating margin baseline.

data.title: How THG Nutrition’s Plex MES Win At Rockwell Automation (ROK) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Rockwell Automation recently announced that THG Nutrition, part of THG PLC and home to Myprotein, has selected the cloud-based Plex Manufacturing Execution System to replace legacy systems, initially rolling it out at its Warrington, UK facility before extending it across multiple global sites.
  • This win highlights how Rockwell’s Plex platform can unify data, improve real-time visibility, and support complex growth-by-acquisition models in highly regulated, multi-site nutrition manufacturing.
  • We’ll now examine how THG Nutrition’s adoption of Plex MES, with its cloud-based standardization benefits, could influence Rockwell Automation’s investment narrative.

Rockwell Automation Investment Narrative Recap

To own Rockwell Automation, you need to believe in continued adoption of industrial automation and cloud software, particularly higher margin MES and SaaS, to justify its premium valuation and heavy investment plans. The THG Nutrition Plex win reinforces Rockwell’s software-led automation story but does not materially change the near term reliance on customers converting delayed CapEx projects or the risk that recurring services growth could soften if clients keep tightening technology budgets.

Among recent developments, Rockwell’s new US$1.5 billion five year unsecured revolving credit facility is worth noting alongside the THG Plex announcement, as it underpins financial flexibility while the company invests about US$2 billion in capacity, digital infrastructure, and talent. For investors, that combination of balance sheet support and software wins like THG Nutrition helps frame how Rockwell is trying to support higher margin growth areas while still being exposed to global CapEx timing and geopolitical uncertainties.

Yet investors should be aware that if large customer CapEx projects remain delayed for longer than expected and do not convert as anticipated, then...

data.title: Will Walker & Dunlop's (WD) New HUD/FHA Platform and Affordable Bridge Venture Reshape Its Narrative?
data.description:

  • This cluster of initiatives signals a deeper push into HUD-insured and affordable multifamily housing services, potentially reinforcing Walker & Dunlop’s role across both valuation and financing in specialized segments of the real estate market.

The recent HUD/FHA valuation launch, affordable housing bridge venture with Pretium, and the Ace Hotel Brooklyn refinancing all fit the theme of deepening in specialized segments, but they do not materially change the immediate sensitivity to transaction volumes and GSE and HUD program dynamics.

Among the latest moves, the US$250 million Walker & Dunlop Affordable Bridge Capital joint venture with Pretium stands out, because it ties directly into affordable multifamily financing, one of the clearest current demand drivers in the story. For investors watching catalysts, this expands Walker & Dunlop’s ability to support projects moving into LIHTC, Section 8, or tax exempt bond programs, while still leaving the business exposed to any shifts in agency or HUD program rules and volumes.

data.title: A Look At Amentum Holdings (AMTM) Valuation After Major Defense Contract Awards And Rising Analyst Optimism
data.description:
Recent wins on a reported $3.5b Defense Threat Reduction Agency contract and a $120m Defense Information Systems Agency award have put Amentum Holdings (AMTM) on investors’ radar as they reassess the stock’s risk reward profile.

See our latest analysis for Amentum Holdings.

The recent contract wins and earnings beats come against a backdrop of strong momentum, with a 90 day share price return of 52.30% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 52.70% from a latest share price of $34.8.

If these contract awards have you rethinking your defense exposure, it might be a good moment to scan aerospace and defense stocks for other names benefiting from similar demand trends.

With the stock up more than 50% over the past year and trading only slightly below the average analyst target, the key question now is whether Amentum is still mispriced value or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Price-to-Earnings of 128.5x: Is it justified?

Amentum closed at $34.80, yet the current P/E of 128.5x stands well above both its peers and the wider US Professional Services industry.

The P/E multiple compares the share price with earnings per share and is a quick way to see how much investors are paying for each dollar of profit. For a services and solutions provider like Amentum, it often reflects how the market assesses the durability and trajectory of earnings.

Here, the signals are mixed. On one side, the company has only recently become profitable, earnings are forecast to grow 31.48% per year and profits have been growing at a high annual rate over the past five years. On the other side, return on equity is currently low at 1.3% and interest payments are not well covered by earnings, which may limit how much of that growth converts into shareholder returns.

Against that backdrop, Amentum's P/E of 128.5x is far above the peer average of 22x, the US Professional Services industry average of 24.6x and an estimated fair P/E of 37x that the market could potentially move toward over time.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Amentum Holdings

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 128.5x (OVERVALUED)

However, you also have to weigh execution and balance sheet risks, given the low return on equity, thin net income of $66 million on $14.393 billion in revenue, and concerns about interest coverage.

Find out about the key risks to this Amentum Holdings narrative.

Another View: What Does The DCF Say?

While the 128.5x P/E screens as expensive, our DCF model suggests an estimated fair value of US$69.63 per share compared to the current US$34.80 price. That indicates Amentum trades at roughly a 50% discount, so which signal do you place more weight on, earnings or cash flows?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

data.title: The Bull Case For Phoenix Financial (TASE:PHOE) Could Change Following Major Convertible Note Issue - Learn Why
data.description:
• Phoenix Financial Ltd recently completed a fixed-income offering of ₪456.22 million in convertible corporate notes priced at 100%, maturing on June 20, 2028.
  • The convertible structure means these notes could eventually be exchanged for equity, affecting Phoenix Financial’s capital mix and potential shareholder dilution.
  • We will now examine how this sizeable convertible bond issue shapes Phoenix Financial’s investment narrative, particularly around balance sheet flexibility and future growth options.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 110 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Phoenix Financial's Investment Narrative?

To own Phoenix Financial, you really need to buy into a story of a profitable insurer using its strong earnings and high return on equity to reinvest, return cash via dividends and buybacks, and still keep its balance sheet resilient. The new ₪456.22 million convertible note issue fits into that picture as a fresh source of funding that could preserve cash in the near term and extend balance sheet flexibility, particularly alongside the existing buyback plans and sizeable recent dividends. In the short run, the key catalysts still revolve around the quality and sustainability of earnings after the large one off gain, plus how actively management leans into repurchases while the share price trades close to consensus targets. The main shift in risk is the added possibility of future dilution if the notes convert, on top of already stretched dividend coverage and a relatively new board that is still proving itself.

However, the potential shareholder dilution from the new convertible notes is easy to overlook at first glance. Phoenix Financial's shares are on the way up, but they could be overextended by 45%. Uncover the fair value now.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Two Simply Wall St Community fair value views for Phoenix range from ₪100.01 to ₪148, underscoring how far apart individual investors can be. data.title: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (MLTX) Is Down 7.4% After FDA Clears HS BLA Path Using Existing Trials
data.description:
• In early January 2026, MoonLake Immunotherapeutics reported that the FDA had provided positive Type B meeting feedback, allowing a Biologics License Application for sonelokimab in hidradenitis suppurativa to proceed in the second half of 2026 using existing MIRA, VELA-1 and VELA-2 trial data without additional HS trials.
  • An important nuance is that while the FDA accepted the overall evidence package and emphasized inclusion of VELA-2 for safety, it ruled out using mechanistic data plus a single trial as confirmatory evidence, shaping how MoonLake must frame efficacy in its filing.
  • We’ll now look at how the FDA’s willingness to consider existing HS trial data without new studies influences MoonLake’s investment narrative.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 22 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

What Is MoonLake Immunotherapeutics' Investment Narrative?

To own MoonLake today, you have to believe that sonelokimab can convert its hidradenitis suppurativa data into an approved, commercially relevant product despite a mixed Phase 3 readout and a company with zero revenue and ongoing losses. The FDA’s Type B feedback is material because it effectively keeps the HS Biologics License Application on its original H2 2026 timetable using MIRA, VELA‑1 and VELA‑2, removing the overhang of an additional costly HS trial while clarifying that mechanistic evidence cannot substitute for a second definitive study. That shifts the near term focus back to regulatory review and readouts in axial spondyloarthritis and psoriatic arthritis, rather than rebuilding the HS program from scratch. The biggest risks now sit around regulatory interpretation of the VELA package, liquidity needs and ongoing shareholder litigation after the steep 2025 share price fall.

However, one key regulatory uncertainty around how the FDA weighs VELA‑2 still hangs over the stock. Our valuation report unveils the possibility MoonLake Immunotherapeutics' shares may be trading at a premium.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The single fair value estimate from the Simply Wall St Community sits at about US$18.63, reflecting just one private investor’s modeling. Set this against MoonLake’s reliance on an HS BLA built on existing trials, where any adverse FDA view on the VELA‑2 data could quickly reshape expectations and reminds you to weigh several different viewpoints before drawing your own conclusion.

data.title: Is Western Gateway’s LA Expansion Quietly Reframing How Phillips 66 (PSX) Integrates Refining and Midstream?
data.description:
• Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan previously launched a second open season for the proposed Western Gateway refined products pipeline, offering remaining capacity, new origin points, and added access to the Los Angeles market through a joint tariff supported by reversing existing Kinder Morgan lines.
  • By linking midcontinent refinery supplies to high-demand markets such as Phoenix, Las Vegas, and Los Angeles, the Western Gateway project reshapes Phillips 66's refined product logistics footprint and could influence how its refining and midstream assets work together.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this expanded Western Gateway access to Los Angeles may affect Phillips 66’s existing investment narrative and risk profile.

We've found 12 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Phillips 66 Investment Narrative Recap

To own Phillips 66 today, you generally need to believe in the value of its integrated refining and midstream network and its ability to keep earning through margin and volume cycles. The Western Gateway expansion toward Los Angeles fits that story by tightening the link between midcontinent refineries and premium demand centers, but it does not fundamentally change the most immediate swing factors, which remain refining margins as a short term catalyst and execution and cost control around asset changes as a key risk.

Against that backdrop, the Western Gateway second open season stands out because it directly relates to Phillips 66’s evolving West Coast footprint as it plans to cease operations at the Los Angeles Refinery by the end of 2025. Added pipeline access into Phoenix, Las Vegas and Los Angeles offers an alternative way to move refined products into Southwest and California markets, which may matter as investors weigh the near term impact of LA-related depreciation and any volume shifts against the potential benefits of a more connected midstream system.

Yet investors should also be aware that, as Phillips 66 reshapes its West Coast presence, the costs tied to closing the Los Angeles Refinery could still...

data.title: A Look At WaterBridge Infrastructure (WBI) Valuation After Recent Share Price Swings
data.description:
With no single headline event driving attention today, investors are watching WaterBridge Infrastructure (WBI) following recent price swings and mixed return patterns over the past week, month, and past three months.

See our latest analysis for WaterBridge Infrastructure.

Over the year to date, WaterBridge Infrastructure’s share price has moved to $21.55, with a 7 day share price return of 6.89% and a 90 day share price return of an 11.75% decline, which suggests recent momentum has cooled after earlier strength.

If this price action has you reviewing the energy space, it could be a useful moment to broaden your watchlist with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With WaterBridge Infrastructure trading at $21.55 against an analyst price target of $28.00 and an indicated intrinsic discount, it is fair to ask: is the stock genuinely undervalued, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Price to Sales of 1.3x: Is it justified?

At the last close of $21.55, WaterBridge Infrastructure is on a P/S of 1.3x, which sits between cheaper peers and a relatively full sector multiple.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value to its revenue and is often used for businesses that are not yet profitable, like WaterBridge Infrastructure. With WBI currently loss making and earning revenue of US$733.296m, investors are effectively paying 1.3 times annual sales for exposure to its water management and related services.

Analysts describe WBI as good value against its peer group on P/S, where the peer average is 2.3x. This suggests the market may be pricing its revenue stream more cautiously than similar companies. Against the broader US Energy Services industry though, WBI screens as expensive. Its 1.3x P/S sits above the 1.2x sector average, which signals that the stock is not outright cheap compared to the wider industry even if it looks attractive relative to its closer peer set.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Sales of 1.3x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, there are still clear watchpoints, including WBI’s current net loss of US$91.252m and the risk that its 14.26% annual revenue growth rate slows or reverses.

Find out about the key risks to this WaterBridge Infrastructure narrative.

Another View: Our DCF Model Paints A Very Different Picture

While the 1.3x P/S ratio points to WaterBridge Infrastructure being roughly in line with the market on sales, our DCF model comes out with a very different signal. On that approach, WBI at US$21.55 is compared with a fair value estimate of US$362.34, implying a very large gap between price and model value. For you, the key question is whether that gap reflects opportunity or optimistic assumptions in the model.

data.description:

See our latest analysis for Ford Motor.

That recent pullback comes after a mixed stretch, with a 1 day share price return of 1.52% decline and a 7 day share price return of 3.06% decline, contrasting with a 90 day share price return of 8.28% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 43.49%. This suggests longer term momentum has been stronger than the latest move implies.

If Ford’s recent swing has you rethinking your auto exposure, this can be a useful moment to scan other auto manufacturers that might fit your portfolio stance.

With Ford trading at $13.60 and sitting only about 0.4% below the average analyst price target of $13.66, the key question is whether the recent 43.49% 1-year return still leaves upside on the table, or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

At a last close of $13.60 versus a most-followed fair value of $12.52, the narrative implies Ford is priced above its estimated worth using a 12.5% discount rate.

The analysts have a consensus price target of $10.8 for Ford Motor based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors. data.title: Tango Therapeutics, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:TNGX) 53% Price Boost Is Out Of Tune With Revenues
data.description:
Tango Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:TNGX) shareholders have had their patience rewarded with a 53% share price jump in the last month. This latest share price bounce rounds out a remarkable 356% gain over the last twelve months.

Following the firm bounce in price, Tango Therapeutics' price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 26.3x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to other companies in the Biotechs industry in the United States, where around half of the companies have P/S ratios below 12.1x and even P/S below 4x are quite common. However, the P/S might be quite high for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

See our latest analysis for Tango Therapeutics

Advertisement

What Does Tango Therapeutics' P/S Mean For Shareholders?

Tango Therapeutics could be doing better as it's been growing revenue less than most other companies lately. It might be that many expect the uninspiring revenue performance to recover significantly, which has kept the P/S ratio from collapsing. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

Keen to find out how analysts think Tango Therapeutics' future stacks up against the industry? In that case, our free report is a great place to start.

How Is Tango Therapeutics' Revenue Growth Trending?

Tango Therapeutics' P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's expected to deliver very strong growth, and importantly, perform much better than the industry.

Taking a look back first, we see that the company grew revenue by an impressive 53% last year. Pleasingly, revenue has also lifted 175% in aggregate from three years ago, thanks to the last 12 months of growth. So we can start by confirming that the company has done a great job of growing revenue over that time.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the nine analysts covering the company suggest revenue should grow by 0.2% per annum over the next three years. With the industry predicted to deliver 126% growth each year, the company is positioned for a weaker revenue result.

With this information, we find it concerning that Tango Therapeutics is trading at a P/S higher than the industry. Apparently many investors in the company are way more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock at any price. There's a good chance these shareholders are setting themselves up for future disappointment if the P/S falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

The Final Word

Shares in Tango Therapeutics have seen a strong upwards swing lately, which has really helped boost its P/S figure. Using the price-to-sales ratio alone to determine if you should sell your stock isn't sensible, however it can be a practical guide to the company's future prospects.

We've concluded that Tango Therapeutics currently trades on a much higher than expected P/S since its forecast growth is lower than the wider industry. The weakness in the company's revenue estimate doesn't bode well for the elevated P/S, which could take a fall if the revenue sentiment doesn't improve. At these price levels, investors should remain cautious, particularly if things don't improve.

We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 3 warning signs for Tango Therapeutics that you need to be mindful of.

If you're unsure about the strength of Tango Therapeutics' business, why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals for some other companies you may have missed.

data.title: Some Confidence Is Lacking In DT Midstream, Inc.'s (NYSE:DTM) P/E
data.description:
DT Midstream, Inc.'s (NYSE:DTM) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 30.4x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to the market in the United States, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 19x and even P/E's below 11x are quite common. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the highly elevated P/E.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

DT Midstream could be doing better as its earnings have been going backwards lately while most other companies have been seeing positive earnings growth. One possibility is that the P/E is high because investors think this poor earnings performance will turn the corner. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

See our latest analysis for DT Midstream

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on DT Midstream.
Advertisement

Is There Enough Growth For DT Midstream?

DT Midstream's P/E ratio would be typical for a company that's expected to deliver very strong growth, and importantly, perform much better than the market.

If we review the last year of earnings, dishearteningly the company's profits fell to the tune of 3.6%. At least EPS has managed not to go completely backwards from three years ago in aggregate, thanks to the earlier period of growth. So it appears to us that the company has had a mixed result in terms of growing earnings over that time.

Looking ahead now, EPS is anticipated to climb by 12% per year during the coming three years according to the nine analysts following the company. With the market predicted to deliver 12% growth each year, the company is positioned for a comparable earnings result.

In light of this, it's curious that DT Midstream's P/E sits above the majority of other companies. Apparently many investors in the company are more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock right now. These shareholders may be setting themselves up for disappointment if the P/E falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

The Bottom Line On DT Midstream's P/E

Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-earnings ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

We've established that DT Midstream currently trades on a higher than expected P/E since its forecast growth is only in line with the wider market. Right now we are uncomfortable with the relatively high share price as the predicted future earnings aren't likely to support such positive sentiment for long. This places shareholders' investments at risk and potential investors in danger of paying an unnecessary premium.

It's always necessary to consider the ever-present spectre of investment risk. We've identified 1 warning sign with DT Midstream, and understanding should be part of your investment process.

Of course, you might also be able to find a better stock than DT Midstream. So you may wish to see this free collection of other companies that have reasonable P/E ratios and have grown earnings strongly.

data.title: Returns Are Gaining Momentum At CEMEX. de (BMV:CEMEXCPO)
data.description:
If we want to find a potential multi-bagger, often there are underlying trends that can provide clues. One common approach is to try and find a company with returns on capital employed (ROCE) that are increasing, in conjunction with a growing amount of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. So when we looked at CEMEX. de (BMV:CEMEXCPO) and its trend of ROCE, we really liked what we saw.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)?

If you haven't worked with ROCE before, it measures the 'return' (pre-tax profit) a company generates from capital employed in its business. The formula for this calculation on CEMEX. de is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.064 = US$1.4b ÷ (US$29b - US$6.5b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Thus, CEMEX. de has an ROCE of 6.4%. In absolute terms, that's a low return and it also under-performs the Basic Materials industry average of 9.2%.

See our latest analysis for CEMEX. de

Above you can see how the current ROCE for CEMEX. de compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering CEMEX. de for free.

What Can We Tell From CEMEX. de's ROCE Trend?

CEMEX. de has not disappointed with their ROCE growth. Looking at the data, we can see that even though capital employed in the business has remained relatively flat, the ROCE generated has risen by 21% over the last five years. Basically the business is generating higher returns from the same amount of capital and that is proof that there are improvements in the company's efficiencies. On that front, things are looking good so it's worth exploring what management has said about growth plans going forward.

What We Can Learn From CEMEX. de's ROCE

To sum it up, CEMEX. de is collecting higher returns from the same amount of capital, and that's impressive. And with a respectable 81% awarded to those who held the stock over the last five years, you could argue that these developments are starting to get the attention they deserve. With that being said, we still think the promising fundamentals mean the company deserves some further due diligence.

While CEMEX. de looks impressive, no company is worth an infinite price. The intrinsic value infographic for CEMEX CPO helps visualize whether it is currently trading for a fair price.

data.title: Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de's (BMV:CHDRAUIB) Returns On Capital Are Heading Higher
data.description:
What are the early trends we should look for to identify a stock that could multiply in value over the long term? Ideally, a business will show two trends; firstly a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an increasing amount of capital employed. Basically this means that a company has profitable initiatives that it can continue to reinvest in, which is a trait of a compounding machine. Speaking of which, we noticed some great changes in Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de's (BMV:CHDRAUIB) returns on capital, so let's have a look.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

If you haven't worked with ROCE before, it measures the 'return' (pre-tax profit) a company generates from capital employed in its business. To calculate this metric for Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de, this is the formula:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.14 = Mex$15b ÷ (Mex$156b - Mex$44b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Thus, Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de has an ROCE of 14%. In absolute terms, that's a pretty standard return but compared to the Consumer Retailing industry average it falls behind.

Check out our latest analysis for Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de

In the above chart we have measured Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de's prior ROCE against its prior performance, but the future is arguably more important. If you're interested, you can view the analysts predictions in our free analyst report for Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de .

The Trend Of ROCE

The trends we've noticed at Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de are quite reassuring. Over the last five years, returns on capital employed have risen substantially to 14%. Basically the business is earning more per dollar of capital invested and in addition to that, 69% more capital is being employed now too. The increasing returns on a growing amount of capital is common amongst multi-baggers and that's why we're impressed.

The Key Takeaway

A company that is growing its returns on capital and can consistently reinvest in itself is a highly sought after trait, and that's what Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de has. Since the stock has returned a staggering 345% to shareholders over the last five years, it looks like investors are recognizing these changes. In light of that, we think it's worth looking further into this stock because if Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de can keep these trends up, it could have a bright future ahead.
